entities.name: pickleball
entities.description: A sport played on a rectangular court with a net.

entities.name: rectangular court
entities.description: The playing area for pickleball, measuring 20 feet wide by 44 feet long.

entities.name: net
entities.description: A horizontal bar suspended between the sidelines of the court.

entities.name: Non-Volley Zone
entities.description: The 14-foot-wide strip in the middle of the court, extending 7 feet from the net on both sides.

entities.name: centerline
entities.description: A line that runs from the kitchen line to the baseline, dividing the court into left and right service areas.

entities.name: baseline
entities.description: The back boundary of the pickleball court.

entities.name: sidelines
entities.description: The outer boundaries on each side of the pickleball court.

entities.name: pickleball paddle
entities.description: A solid, flat surface made from rigid materials used to hit the ball.

entities.name: perforated polymer ball
entities.description: The ball used in pickleball with specific size and weight requirements.

entities.name: athletic footwear
entities.description: Footwear worn by players, recommended to have non-marking soles.

entities.name: Serve
entities.description: A fundamental aspect of pickleball governed by specific rules.

entities.name: Underhand Motion
entities.description: The serving motion in pickleball where the paddle is below waist level.

entities.name: Baseline
entities.description: A line marking the back boundary of a pickleball court.

entities.name: Service Court
entities.description: One of two designated areas on a pickleball court where serves must be made and returned.

entities.name: Drop Serve
entities.description: An alternative serve option in pickleball introduced in 2021.

entities.name: Paddle
entities.description: The implement used to hit the ball in pickleball.

entities.name: Net
entities.description: A horizontal bar that separates the two halves of a pickleball court.

entities.name: two-bounce rule
entities.description: A specific rule in pickleball that requires both the serve and return to bounce before a volley can be hit.

entities.name: pickleball
entities.description: A sport played with a paddle and a small ball on a badminton court.

entities.name: Non-Volley Zone
entities.description: A 7-foot zone on each side of the net where aggressive net play is prohibited.

entities.name: Kitchen Line
entities.description: The boundary line that defines the Non-Volley Zone.

entities.name: Volley
entities.description: Hitting the ball out of the air before it bounces.

entities.name: Momentum Rule
entities.description: Preventing players from entering the kitchen due to momentum from a volley motion.

entities.name: Dinking
entities.description: A soft shot that lands in the opponent's Non-Volley Zone, forcing them to hit up on the ball.

entities.name: Toeing the Line
entities.description: Standing as close to the Kitchen Line as possible without touching it for better reach and control.

entities.name: Pickleball
entities.description: A racquet sport played on a badminton court with a small pickleball and a paddle.

entities.name: Side-out scoring system
entities.description: The standard scoring method in pickleball where only the serving team can score points.

entities.name: Rally scoring
entities.description: An experimental scoring method introduced by USAPA where a point is scored on every rally, regardless of which team is serving.

entities.name: Games to 11 points
entities.description: The standard game format in pickleball where games are played to 11 points with a win-by-2 rule.

entities.name: Games to 15 or 21 points
entities.description: Extended game formats used in some tournaments and recreational play where games are played to 15 or 21 points with a win-by-2 rule.

entities.name: Best-of-three format
entities.description: A tournament format where the first team to win two games wins the match.

entities.name: Singles scoring
entities.description: Scoring method in singles pickleball where the score is announced with just two numbers: the server's score and the receiver's score.

entities.name: Doubles scoring
entities.description: Scoring method in doubles pickleball where the score is announced with three numbers: the serving team's score, the receiving team's score, and the server number (1 or 2).

entities.name: Server
entities.description: The player who serves the ball in pickleball.

entities.name: Receiver
entities.description: The player who receives the serve in pickleball.

entities.name: Fault
entities.description: A mistake in pickleball that results in losing the rally.

entities.name: Ball out of bounds
entities.description: The ball landing outside the court boundaries is a fault.

entities.name: Ball into the net
entities.description: The ball hitting the net and not clearing it to the opponent's side is a fault.

entities.name: Volleying from the non-volley zone
entities.description: Volleys made while standing in the kitchen or if momentum carries into the kitchen are faults.

entities.name: Serve rule violations
entities.description: Faults on serves including hitting above waist level, stepping on baseline, not serving diagonally, and ball not clearing net.

entities.name: Ball hitting a player
entities.description: The ball hitting a player before it bounces is a fault except for the paddle hand below the wrist.

entities.name: Two-bounce violation
entities.description: Volleying the ball before the two-bounce rule has been satisfied is a fault.

entities.name: Double hit
entities.description: The ball being hit twice by the same player or team before crossing the net is a fault, except for hitting paddle and then hand in one motion.

entities.name: Carrying or scooping the ball
entities.description: The ball must be cleanly hit, not carried or scooped on the paddle.

entities.name: Serving out of turn
entities.description: In doubles, serving when it is not your turn is a fault, and points scored during that serve are lost.

entities.name: doubles pickleball
entities.description: A variant of pickleball played with two teams of two players each.

entities.name: kitchen line
entities.description: The optimal position on the court for hitting downward volleys and controlling the game pace.

entities.name: communication
entities.description: Effective communication between partners to coordinate shots, poaching, and strategic movements.

entities.name: stacking
entities.description: A formation where both players stand on the same side of the court to keep a player's forehand in the middle or allow a stronger player to cover more of the court.

entities.name: poaching
entities.description: When the non-hitting partner crosses in front of their partner to hit a volley, an aggressive strategy that requires good timing and communication.

entities.name: third shot drop
entities.description: A soft shot hit by the serving team after the serve and return, landing in the kitchen to allow transition from the baseline to the kitchen line.

entities.name: dinking
entities.description: Hitting soft shots that land in the opponent's kitchen, forcing them to hit up on the ball.
